name: Gredas de Bolnuevo y Puntas Calnegre via Eurovelo 8 — gravel round trip from Puerto de Mazarrón
name_original: Gredas de Bolnuevo y Puntas Calnegre por Eurovelo 8 — gravel ida y vuelta desde Puerto de Mazarrón
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Mazarrón offer diverse landscapes, from coastal paths along the Mediterranean Sea to inland trails through agricultural areas and protected mountain ranges. The region is characterized by its varied terrain, including unique sandstone formations like the Bolnuevo Erosions and the well-maintained Vía Verde. Coastal mountain ranges such as the Sierra de la Almenara and Sierra de las Moreras provide elevated routes with panoramic views. This combination of features caters to a range of cycling preferences, from flat coastal rides to more challenging ascents. Best touring cycling routes around Mazarrón The most popular touring cycling route is Gredas de Bolnuevo y Puntas Calnegre via Eurovelo 8 — gravel round trip from Puerto de Mazarrón, a 13.1 miles (21.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 49 minutes to complete, offering coastal views and unique rock formations. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Territorio Tortuga Mural – Cueva del Agua loop from Playa de Bolnuevo, an easy 18.0 miles (29.0 km) path. This route provides scenic coastal views and passes by cultural points. Local touring cyclists also love the Desert path with palm trees – Moreras Mountain Range loop from Mazarrón, a 19.8 miles (31.9 km) trail leading through inland scenery and the Sierra de las Moreras, often completed in about 2 hours 24 minutes. Touring cycling around Mazarrón is defined by coastal paths, mountain trails, and the Vía Verde. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy coastal loops to moderate climbs in the hills. The routes in Mazarrón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 1000 reviews. More than 5000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Mazarrón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Mazarrón?
A: Mazarrón offers a wide variety of touring cycling routes, with over 150 options available. These routes cater to different skill levels, including 53 easy, 43 moderate, and 55 difficult trails, ensuring there's something for every cyclist.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Mazarrón?
A: Mazarrón, located on Spain's Costa Cálida, benefits from a mild Mediterranean climate, making it suitable for touring cycling almost year-round. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures, ideal for longer rides. Even winter provides good conditions, though some coastal trails might be unpaved and challenging due to weather.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Mazarrón?
A: You can expect a diverse range of terrain in Mazarrón. Routes vary from stunning coastal paths with sea views, like those incorporating the EuroVelo 8, to inland trails through agricultural areas and protected mountain ranges such as the Sierra de la Almenara and Sierra de las Moreras. There's also the relatively flat and well-conditioned Vía Verde for a smoother ride.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Mazarrón?
A: The touring cycling routes in Mazarrón are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 1000 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from picturesque coastal views to challenging mountain ascents, and the unique natural features like the Bolnuevo Erosions.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Mazarrón?
A: Yes, Mazarrón offers several easy touring cycling routes perfect for beginners and families. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19388758" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Territorio Tortuga Mural – Cueva del Agua loop from Playa de Bolnuevo&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 18.0-mile (29.0 km) path with scenic coastal views. The Vía Verde also provides a flat, accessible option.

Q: Are there challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders in Mazarrón?
A: Experienced riders looking for a challenge will find plenty of options, particularly in the region's mountain ranges. Routes through the Sierra de las Moreras, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27633065" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Desert path with palm trees – Moreras Mountain Range loop from Mazarrón&lt;/a&gt;, offer moderate climbs and rewarding vistas. For even more demanding rides, the nearby Sierra Espuña natural park provides longer routes up to 100 km with significant elevation gains.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in Mazarrón?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Mazarrón are designed as loops or round trips, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016745439/gredas-de-bolnuevo-y-puntas-calnegre-via-eurovelo-8-gravel-round-trip-from-puerto-de-mazarron" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gredas de Bolnuevo y Puntas Calnegre via Eurovelo 8 — gravel round trip from Puerto de Mazarrón&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40371524" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Águilas Marina – Bolnuevo Sandstone Formations loop from Mazarrón&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along touring cycling routes in Mazarrón?
A: Mazarrón's routes are rich with natural beauty and historical sites. You can cycle past the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/998557" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bolnuevo Sandstone Formations&lt;/a&gt;, explore the Mediterranean landscape of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5416272" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moreras Mountain Range&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy views from summits like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1839688" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vipers' Hill – Morro Blanco&lt;/a&gt;. The coastline also features unspoiled coves and beaches like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7156224" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Percheles Beach&lt;/a&gt;. Historical sites such as the Roman Salt Preserving Factory Museum can also be incorporated into your tours.

Q: Is there a dedicated cycling path like a Vía Verde in Mazarrón?
A: Yes, Mazarrón features a branch of the Vía Verde network. This 14-kilometer greenway connects Mazarrón and La Pinilla, utilizing former railway lines. It offers a relatively flat and well-conditioned path, making it an excellent option for a relaxed touring cycling experience.

Q: Where can I find parking for touring cycling routes in Mazarrón?
A: Parking is generally available in key areas such as Puerto de Mazarrón and Mazarrón town, which serve as common starting points for many routes. Specific parking information for individual routes can often be found in the route descriptions on komoot. For more general information on active tourism in the area, you can visit &lt;a href="https://visitamazarron.com/en/mazarron/active-tourism/bike-touring/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;visitamazarron.com&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there cafes or pubs along the touring cycling routes in Mazarrón?
A: While specific cafes and pubs directly on every trail might vary, routes often pass through or near towns and villages like Puerto de Mazarrón, Bolnuevo, and Mazarrón itself, where you can find various establishments to refuel and relax. Coastal routes, in particular, offer opportunities to stop at beachside chiringuitos.

Q: Can I combine touring cycling with other outdoor activities in Mazarrón?
A: Absolutely! Mazarrón is a hub for outdoor activities. After a ride, you can relax on one of the 35 km of unspoiled beaches, explore marine reserves for diving, or enjoy hiking trails like the climb to Morrón Blanco in the Sierra de las Moreras. The region's diverse landscape makes it perfect for multi-activity holidays.
tips: A difficult section with the touring bike, so plan your time. It's a horrible off-road track that needs maintenance. With full luggage you sometimes have to push the bike. But the bays are very beautiful and the view of the sea makes up for it.

At this point we are in the viewpoint of the swing. From here we will have magnificent views of Playa del Rincón and beautiful sunsets. In the gazebo there is a swing that makes the place even more entertaining.

The natural monument also called Ciudad Encantada de Bolnuevo is one of the main attractions of the Region of Murcia. This wonder is the result of the action of wind and water over the years. If you look carefully you will find some surprises like fossils.
